countdown.title: Counting Down to Our Special Day

story.title: Our Story
story.subtitle: How It All Began
paragraphs: Our story began at Qorikancha in Cusco, Peru — an ancient Incan temple where two travellers from Asia happened to cross paths. We noticed each other right away, the only Asian faces in the crowd, but we didn't exchange a single word.

Fate had other plans. Weeks later, we both checked into Hotel Prada, a cozy guesthouse in Lima. This time, we introduced ourselves properly and spent the days sharing meals, drinking coffee, and exploring the streets of Lima together.

We weren't ready to say goodbye. We decided to travel to Quito, Ecuador, where we stood together at the equator and wandered through the beautiful historic center. From there, we headed to Colombia, where we learned Spanish and danced salsa together.

Our adventures continued across the Americas — exploring the streets of New York City, standing in awe at Niagara Falls, and discovering countless places along the way. A few months later, we reunited in Nagoya, Japan, wandering through the city together.
Then came Hong Kong — Man Man's hometown — where Ken got to experience her world, her favourite places, and the city she grew up in.

After all the travels, all the conversations, and all the moments shared across continents, Ken knew without a doubt — Man Man was the perfect partner for life. Now, we're ready to begin our greatest adventure yet, and we want you to be there when we say "I do."

dressCode.title: Dress Code
dressCode.subtitle: What to Wear
dressCode.description: Korean weddings follow a semi-formal to formal dress code. Please dress elegantly to match the occasion.

suggestions.title: Suggestions
items: Ladies: Elegant blouses with skirts, or dressy separates
Gentlemen: Suits or dress shirts with slacks and dress shoes
Please avoid: Jeans, sneakers, casual wear, shorts, flip-flops, or white outfits (reserved for the bride)
